business.headline: India-US trade deal very close, but here's why it isn't signed yetPiyush Goyal said India and the US are close to finalising their trade deal. India wants tariff terms that give its exporters a clear edge over rivals.

Dalal Street pares gains: Sensex ends flat after climbing over 600 pointsEarly optimism driven by lower crude oil prices and a stronger rupee faded as investors booked profits, though benchmark indices still managed to end in positive territory.

Zerodha's Nithin Kamath has a warning for you against pyramid schemesAccording to figures cited by Kamath, more than 5.5 crore Indians have lost their savings to over 5,300 such schemes. The estimated losses stood at around Rs 10 lakh crore as of 2015, and he suggested that the actual figure could be much higher now.

Gold loses over Rs 51,000 from all-time high. Why is the yellow metal crashing?Gold has fallen sharply from its peak as silver also comes under selling pressure. The correction is being driven by rate-hike bets, a stronger dollar and shifting investor appetite.

United Spirits may cut 100 jobs as Diageo looks to trim costsUnited Spirits is set to lay off around 100 employees as Diageo pursues a global cost-cutting programme. The move comes amid a wider restructuring drive in India despite strong growth and continued investment in premium brands.

Amazon to invest $48 billion in India by 2030, CEO Andy Jassy meets PM ModiAccording to Jassy, the company aims to support 3.8 million jobs in India by 2030 and help generate $80 billion in cumulative e-commerce exports.

Sensex jumps over 600 points: Why are stock markets rallying today?Benchmark indices extended their recovery on Thursday as softer crude, firm Asian cues and gains in banks and autos lifted sentiment. The move has eased near-term macro concerns, though investors remain watchful of the monsoon and global central bank signals.

EPFO portal shutdown from June 26-28: How it will affect your EPF claimsPlanning to withdraw money from your EPF account or submit a claim this week? You may have to postpone it. EPFO has announced a three-day shutdown of key portal services as it upgrades its claims processing system.

GTA 5 made $10 billion. Here's what GTA 6 could earn if it repeats historyGrand Theft Auto V is the second best-selling video game ever made. As pre-orders open for its successor, here is what the numbers say about how big GTA 6 could really be.

7 reasons why you must file ITR even if your income falls below exemption limitThink your income is too low to require an ITR? Not necessarily. Certain financial transactions can make return filing mandatory even if your income is below the exemption limit.

Explained: Why this investor calls India's stock market a poison marketAfter years of making money in the stock market, ace investor Shankar Sharma has delivered one of his strongest warnings yet. Here's why he believes Dalal Street is becoming a poison market.

Oil at pre-Iran war lows lifts Dalal Street; Sensex and Nifty jumpThe S&P BSE Sensex climbed 447.36 points, or 0.58%, to 77,438.58, while the NSE Nifty50 advanced 136.90 points, or 0.57%, to 24,158.55 as of 9:28 am.

RBI says banks must notify pensioners before recovering excess pensionThe RBI has told banks to notify government pensioners before recovering any excess pension paid into their accounts. The amendment seeks to stop sudden debits and set a clearer recovery process through notice and consent.

Lost money to online fraud? RBI's new rule might just save youThe RBI has set new rules on who bears losses from fraudulent electronic banking transactions. Customers who report quickly can get full reversals or capped compensation under a time-bound process.

Stock mismatch, mega offshore deals: What ED found during raids on Rajesh ExportsThe Enforcement Directorate searched nine premises linked to Rajesh Exports in Bengaluru and Mumbai under FEMA. The agency says missing records, stock gaps, trade set-offs and suspicious trades are now under investigation.

SK Hynix surpasses Samsung as South Korea's most valuable company amid AI chip boomSK Hynix has emerged as one of the biggest beneficiaries thanks to its leadership in high-bandwidth memory (HBM) chips, a key component used in advanced AI systems.

Elon Musk no longer a trillionaire as SpaceX, Tesla shares declineThe decline in Musk's fortune came after investors sold technology stocks amid concerns about an artificial intelligence-driven market bubble and the possibility of higher interest rates.

From inspections to intelligent compliance: The next step in implementing the ESI ActIn the final part of this series on labour laws and Inspector Raj, this article examines why expanding social-security coverage is only half the battle, and how risk-based inspections, data analytics and AI-driven compliance could help ensure that workers actually receive the protections guaranteed under the ESI Act.

Why the Calcutta Stock Exchange has been shut for over a decadeIndia's oldest regional stock exchange has not seen a single trade since 2013. With West Bengal now proposing to revive it, here is exactly why it shut down in the first place.

Sensex ends 790 points higher, Nifty tops 24,000; IndiGo up 5%The S&P BSE Sensex climbed 790.54 points, or 1.04%, to close at 76,991.22, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 197.55 points, or 0.83%, to settle at 24,021.65.

entertainment.headline: Ananthan Kaadu review: Arya's revenge thriller drowns in its own sub-plotsRelease Date:Thu Jun 25, 2026 Ananthan Kaadu movie review: Director Jiyen Krishnakumar's Ananthan Kaadu, starring Arya, Murali Gopy, Indrans and Vijayaraghavan, is a story about how the system neverlets you win. The film has an interesting backdrop, but fails to achieve a cohesive story.

OTT releases this week: Top picks to watch on Prime Video, JioHotstar, SonyLIV This week's OTT slate brings Perfect Family, Lingam, The Bear Season 4 and Alliance to streaming platforms. The mix spans family conflict, crime, acclaimed international drama and strategy-led reality television.

Pathaan vs Chauhaan in Ajay Devgn's Kashmir-based film, first look out Ajay Devgn's Kashmir-based action film Chauhaan has been announced with a first-look video and a punchline aimed at Pathaans. The tribute-led reveal marks his first collaboration with Aanand L Rai and has drawn attention online.

Prahaar teaser: Rajkummar Rao as Ujjwal Nikam puts Kasab trial back in focus The teaser of Prahaar-The Ujjwal Nikam Story introduces Rajkummar Rao as public prosecutor Ujjwal Nikam and centres on the Ajmal Kasab trial after the 26/11 attacks.

What's the update on the 3 Idiots sequel? Rajkumar Hirani reveals new details Rajkumar Hirani said the 3 Idiots sequel is in an early writing stage, with Aamir Khan liking the initial idea. The film will revisit Rancho, Farhan and Raju.

Indian Idol rejected Deepali Sahay is now viral voice of Tere Paas Main | Interview Singer Deepali Sahay spoke about landing Tere Paas Main with AR Rahman and revisiting Indian Idol. She said the song marks years of persistence and reflects her push to change views on Bhojpuri music.

Rajkumar Hirani defends Dunki, internet asks him to stop blaming the audience Director Rajkumar Hirani has defended Dunki, saying its story of illegal immigration had a narrower appeal in India. He said viewers abroad connected more deeply with the film because they understood the hardships and regret tied to migration.

Alka Yagnik reacts to viral wheelchair video, assures fans amid health concern Alka Yagnik addressed concern after a wheelchair video from the Padma ceremony went viral. She said she was tired after the event and is recovering well.

CM Vijay's bus ride wins hearts, fans cheer as he poses with conductor. Video Vijay flagged off 300 new government buses in Chennai and rode one of the new services. The event underlined his government's push to strengthen public transport as clips spread online.

House of the Dragon's Olivia Cooke, 32, shocked to play mom to 31-year-old Tom Actor Olivia Cooke opened up about her surprise at playing the mother of a co-star just a year younger than her in House of the Dragon, sparking fresh discussion about casting and ageing in Hollywood.

Actor Ravi Mohan says he'll work tirelessly weeks after quitting cinema remark Actor Ravi Mohan has thanked fans for their support and said he is getting stronger every day. The message comes after his recent remarks about quitting cinema and signals renewed resolve.

Suriya, Jyotika review Malayalam 'masterpiece' Balan: The Boy, call it a gem Actors Jyotika and Suriya praised Chidambaram's Balan: The Boy after meeting the film's team. While reviewing the film, Suriya called it a "gem of Malayalam film industry."

Gram Chikitsalay vs Panchayat: Why they feel same same, but different Viewers quickly compared Gram Chikitsalay with Panchayat as another outsider-in-a-village story. The similarities end there, with one show centred on governance and the other on rural healthcare.

6 must-watch OTT releases this week on Netflix From the historical epic Raja Shivaji and the return of Lock Upp to Korean psychological drama Notes from the Last Row, Netflix has a packed slate of releases this week. The lineup spans action, reality TV, comedy and fantasy, offering something for every kind of viewer.

Forget the action, Samantha's Maa Inti Bangaaram also champions female friendship In Maa Inti Bangaaram, Samantha's Swarna turns to Manjusha's Kiranmayi when her life spirals into chaos. Their bond drives a story about acceptance, loyalty and women choosing each other.

Inside Alka Yagnik's wealth: How she built her fortune over four decades in music Alka Yagnik received the Padma Bhushan from President Droupadi Murmu in Delhi. The honour has renewed focus on her long music career, health struggle and high-value assets.

90 takes, 20-min breaks: Prithviraj describes a day on Rajamouli's Varanasi set Prithviraj Sukumaran recalled a Varanasi scene with Mahesh Babu that ran through more than 90 takes under SS Rajamouli. He said the filmmaker's exacting routine, from 20-minute lunch breaks to early arrivals, reflected relentless commitment on set.

Millie Bobby Brown says she cried and battled depression after Stranger Things Actor Millie Bobby Brown opened up about the emotional toll of saying goodbye to Stranger Things after a decade. The actor revealed how the end of the hit series affected her in unexpected ways.

Ram Charan inaugurates cricket stadium amid fans' deafening Peddi chants. Watch Ram Charan inaugurated the ACA International Cricket Stadium at Andhra Pradesh's Mangalagiri as fans chanted Peddi across the venue. The launch came during the Andhra Premier League's decisive stage.

general news.headline: Why Khamenei funeral invite for PM Modi puts India in a delicate spotIran has invited Prime Minister Narendra Modi to attend Ayatollah Ali Khamenei's state funeral. How New Delhi responds to the invitation could reveal how it plans to balance ties with Iran, the US and Israel. It's a tightrope walk for New Delhi for sure.

Siya Goyal, boyfriend planned fiance's murder at Pune cafe day before killing himCCTV footage shows Siya Goyal and her boyfriend Chetan Chaudhary at a Pune cafe a day before Ketan Agarwal's death. Police allege the duo discussed the murder plan and used YouTube videos to study Lohagad Fort before carrying out the conspiracy.

Why police verification for passport if it proves nothing? 5 big questionsAn MEA official reiterated that a passport is a "travel document" and not a "citizenship document". The clarification settles one debate about the passport, but opens up five larger questions about verification, ownership, surrender, and citizenship itself.

In a first, Canada says Khalistanis planted Air India bomb. Why this is big for IndiaFor the first time, Canada's intelligence agency has explicitly blamed Khalistani extremists for the 1985 Air India bombing — an attack New Delhi has long described as a Khalistani terror plot. The acknowledgement marks a major shift in Ottawa's stance and could reshape how Canada addresses Khalistani extremism and its ties with India.

Videos capture apocalyptic scenes after shattering Venezuela earthquakesVenezuela Earthquake Videos: Social media platforms were flooded with videos from across Venezuela, showing panicked residents fleeing into streets as buildings shook violently. One widely circulated clip from an airport showed the entire terminal swaying, with passengers and staff running for cover as the tremors intensified.

Rahul Gandhi seeks relief in defamation case involving Shivraj Singh Chouhan's sonRahul Gandhi has moved the Madhya Pradesh High Court in a defamation case over his 2018 remarks against Shivraj Singh Chouhan's son. The petition expresses regret and says the statement was not about Kartikeya Singh ahead of Thursday's hearing.

Passport not proof of citizenship, says govt. Why it shouldn't come as surpriseThe Centre has reiterated that an Indian passport is not conclusive proof of citizenship. It should not come as a surprise. The legal position has been settled for decades. It has revived questions on which documents can legally establish citizenship.

Bhagwant Mann denies sacrilege video, claims masked impostor framed himPunjab Chief Minister Bhagwant Mann dismissed the viral video as fake, alleging it featured a masked impersonator as part of a political conspiracy to defame him on religious grounds.
Citizenship of India: How you have it, how you don'tCitizenship of India is a nice thought, a happy notion and a grand legal nuance. You think you have it, but do you, really?

48-min CCTV trail, Siya's changing versions: How Ketan's fall became murder probePolice are re-examining Ketan Agarwal's death at Lohagad Fort after CCTV footage and digital records raised doubts over the accidental fall account. The probe has widened around alleged inconsistencies, possible coordination and an unclear timeline at the scene.

Is Varanasi becoming the world's second vegetarian city?Varanasi Municipal Corporation's decision to relocate meat, fish and poultry shops to the city's outskirts has triggered debate over access and livelihoods in the temple city. Officials said it would promote cleanliness and spirituality, while residents argued that the move could reshape their diets.

Live: Toll in Venezuela earthquakes rises to 164, 971 injured; power supply hitVenezuela Earthquake News: Two strong earthquakes struck Venezuela within less than a minute early on Thursday, causing extensive damage and prompting evacuations in Caracas. The first quake, measured at magnitude 7.1 by the US Geological Survey, originated near the coastal town of Moron, about 168 kilometres west of the capital, at a shallow depth of 13 kilometres. Around 40 seconds later, a stronger 7.5-magnitude tremor hit the same area at a depth of 10 kilometres, worsening the destruction. Buildings suffered structural damage, walls collapsed, and clouds of dust spread across affected neighbourhoods as emergency teams began rescue and assessment operations. Authorities warned that injuries were likely and cautioned residents about possible aftershocks. Officials described the event as the strongest seismic activity to affect the country in roughly a century. Tsunami alerts issued for Puerto Rico and the Virgin Islands were later withdrawn. Follow IndiaToday.in for the latest updates.

Alka Yagnik reacts to viral wheelchair video, assures fans amid health concernAlka Yagnik addressed concern after a wheelchair video from the Padma ceremony went viral. She said she was tired after the event and is recovering well.

India to restart tourist visas for Bangladeshi nationals from June 28India will resume issuing tourist visas to Bangladeshi nationals from June 28 through five visa centres, ending a nearly two-year suspension imposed after attacks on its diplomatic missions during the 2024 unrest.

Why Bengal's school meal debate is really about eggs versus soyWest Bengal's move to let the ISKCON-linked Annamitra Foundation run midday meals has sparked a row over replacing eggs with vegetarian proteins. The dispute has widened into a debate on nutrition quality and the role of religious dietary norms in a public scheme.

Krishna means river, not religion: NCERT denies bias in Class 6 Kannada textbookNCERT has rejected allegations of religious and dietary bias in its Class 6 Kannada textbook, clarifying that "Krishna" refers to the river and not religion. The council said the book promotes balanced nutrition and inclusive learning aligned with NEP 2020.

When IIT job offers vanish: Inside the biggest trust crisis in campus placementsThe issue is no longer the withdrawn offers themselves. It is whether an IIT offer letter still carries the certainty it once did. The answer will determine not just the fate of one placement season, but the future credibility of India's most trusted campus recruitment system.

In phone industry, everyone is crying RAM RAM and it isn't stopping anytime soonThe smartphone industry is in a state of crisis for phone makers and consumers. And the problem is that of RAM, or rather the price of RAM.

POK cries for food, medicine as Pak imposes blockade to punish it for protestsAs food, fuel and medicine shortages worsen across POK, residents and opposition parties are accusing Islamabad of restricting the flow of essential goods into the region. Pakistani officials have denied the allegations, but reports suggest authorities have disrupted supplies to quell the growing protest movement in the illegally-occupied territory.

At what price does E85 start making sense? The economics behind ethanol fuelE85 fuel is cheaper at the pump, but its lower energy density increases consumption. So, at what price does it actually become cheaper to run than E20? We crunch the numbers.

Monumental mess? Lionel Messi's 85-foot statue in Argentina divides fansArgentina has unveiled an 85-foot Lionel Messi statue in Cutral Co. Its trophy placement and unfinished lower half have triggered ridicule online, even as locals celebrate the monument's tourist pull.

Thousands feared dead after monster Venezuela earthquakes; cities left in ruins Live UpdatesSurvivors described scenes "like a horror movie" after twin earthquakes -- the most powerful to strike Venezuela in over a century -- devastated parts of the Latin American country. Buildings collapsed, families searched through rubble for loved ones, and rescuers worked through the night amid fears of high casualties.

Amazon to invest $48 billion in India by 2030, CEO Andy Jassy meets PM ModiAccording to Jassy, the company aims to support 3.8 million jobs in India by 2030 and help generate $80 billion in cumulative e-commerce exports.

Anand Mahindra moved by 80-year-old violinist playing on Kolkata streets, offers helpFor years, Bhagwan Mallick has played the violin on the streets of Kolkata to earn a living. Now, the 80-year-old musician's story has caught the attention of industrialist Anand Mahindra, who has offered to help.

sports.headline: Trouble for India? Australia may get huge boost for high-voltage Lord's clash Women's T20 World Cup: Australia head coach Shelley Nitschke said that Phoebe Litchfield may return against India on June 28 at the Lord's, saying that that left-handed batter has recovered well from her calf injury.

Asian Games silver medallist Yarraji targets gold after comeback from ACL injury Jyothi Yarraji marked her return from an ACL tear with a 12.99-second hurdles win at the National Inter-State Championships. A year after injury and surgery, India's record holder now believes Asian Games gold is within reach.

Why Jannik Sinner wore a cooling vest at training ahead of Wimbledon Jannik Sinner used a cooling vest during Wimbledon preparations due to the London heatwave, which has also impacted tournament operations, causing a Hawk-Eye outage and drawing criticism from players including Eva Lys.

Monumental mess? Lionel Messi's 85-foot statue in Argentina divides fans Argentina has unveiled an 85-foot Lionel Messi statue in Cutral Co. Its trophy placement and unfinished lower half have triggered ridicule online, even as locals celebrate the monument's tourist pull.

Tennis players want more money, and they are scapegoating media for it The prize money controversy from the French Open is set to boil over to another Grand Slam. In a show of defiance, top tennis players in the world are set to limit their press interactions to 15 minutes at Wimbledon.

Manika Batra's Asian Games doors remain shut: Table tennis federation source The Table Tennis Federation of India have shut the doors on Manika Batra's selection to the main squad for the Asian Games. TTFI sources claim that due process was followed during the selection process.

Food poisoning or what? Korean media tear into coach after shock World Cup loss South Korea manager Hong Myung-bo came under heavy criticism from the country's media after his side's lifeless 0-1 defeat to South Africa. The loss left them staring at an early FIFA World Cup exit.

Neymar's son mistaken for pitch invader, Brazil star steps in to clear confusion FIFA World Cup 2026: Before sharing a heartwarming hug with his father, Neymar's son, Davi Lucca, was stopped from entering the field after Brazil's 3-0 win over Scotland in Miami. Stewards mistook him for a pitch invader, but the Brazil star quickly stepped in to clear up the confusion.

Watch: India 4x100 women beat China in China for gold in Asian relay meet India's women's 4x100m relay team delivered a tactical masterclass to stun hosts China and clinch Gold at the Asian Relay Championships. It was a big boost to the quartet of SS Sneha, Sudeshna Shivankar, Tamanna, and veteran anchor Srabani Nanda ahead of a busy season.

World Cup legend Guillermo Ochoa gets storybook ending, retires at the Azteca Guillermo Ochoa got the farewell he had dreamed of at the Azteca Stadium as Mexico completed a perfect FIFA World Cup group-stage campaign with a 3-0 win over Czechia. Making what is expected to be his final World Cup appearance, the cult hero soaked in the applause of more than 80,000 fans who stood to celebrate one of the tournament's most recognisable figures.

Meet Srabani Nanda: The 35-year-old sprinter who refuses to slow downAt 35, veteran Odisha sprinter Srabani Nanda is redefining longevity in Indian athletics, anchoring the national relay team to fresh continental success in China while championing a fierce, lifelong commitment to clean sport.

From accidental goalkeeper to India's best: Bichu Devi dreams of LA28 gloryFrom taking up hockey and goalkeeping via accident, Bichu Devi Kharibam established herself as India's best shotstopper in hockey at the moment. With LA Olympics dream in mind, the 25-year-old aims to continue growing under the tutelage of Savita Punia.

FIFA World Cup: South Africa achieve historic knockout qualificationSouth Africa scripted history at the FIFA World Cup after beating South Korea in their final group-stage match to qualify for the knockout rounds for the first time. Thapelo Maseko scored the decisive goal in the second half as Hugo Broos' side overcame a difficult start to the tournament.

Wyatt-Hodge helps England reach semis with West Indies winDanni Wyatt-Hodge drove England to a 38-run win over West Indies at Lord's and into the semi-finals. The result avenged England's 2024 group-stage loss and left West Indies needing to beat Ireland to qualify.

981 days in injury exile, Neymar brought to tears on Brazil returnBrazil samba-ed their way into the knockouts of the FIFA World Cup with a statement 3-0 win against Scotland. Neymar Jr. walked into the pitch for the national team after an injury-exile of 981 days.

T20 World Cup: Bruised India face tricky Bangladesh test in must-win gameWomen's T20 World Cup, IND vs BAN: India meet Bangladesh in a must-win Women's T20 World Cup match at Old Trafford after defeat to South Africa. The result could turn on India's fragile middle order, Bangladesh's spinners and the pressure of qualification.

Morocco overcomes Haiti scare to enter WC 2026 knockouts as Group C runners-upMorocco beat Haiti 4-2 in an instant World Cup classic to finish second in Group C. In a match that saw four goals in the first half, the Atlas Lions were able to battle on score two in the final 45 minutes to get the victory.

Neymar's return, Vini Jr's night: Brazil head to knockouts with 3-0 win vs ScotlandSCO vs BRA, FIFA World Cup 2026: Vinicius Jr scored twice as Brazil beat Scotland 3-0 to top Group C, while Neymar made his long-awaited return from injury in front of a packed Miami Stadium to cap a memorable night.

KOR 0-1 RSA Highlights: South Africa achieve history, qualify for knockoutsSouth Korea vs South Africa, FIFA World Cup 2026 Highlights: South Africa have achieved a historic qualification to the knockout round, courtesy of Maseko's second half goal against South Korea. Meanwhile, Mexico stay perfect in the group stage and hammer Czechia 3-0 in their final group stage game.

Switzerland deny Canada top spot, but hosts seal historic World Cup knockout berthSUI vs CAN, FIFA World Cup 2026: Canada's World Cup dream rolls on despite a 2-1 defeat to Switzerland, with the co-hosts reaching the knockout stage for the first time in history as the Swiss clinched top spot in Group B.

Bosnia knock Qatar out of World Cup with 3-1 win to keep knockout hopes aliveBosnia and Herzegovina beat Qatar 3-1 in Seattle to keep their World Cup campaign alive. The result knocked Qatar out and left Bosnia relying on other groups to reach the Round of 32 for the first time in their history.

Switzerland deny Canada top spot, but hosts seal historic World Cup knockout berthSUI vs CAN, FIFA World Cup 2026: Canada's World Cup dream rolls on despite a 2-1 defeat to Switzerland, with the co-hosts reaching the knockout stage for the first time in history as the Swiss clinched top spot in Group B.

SCO vs BRA Highlights: Vini Jr shines for Brazil, Morocco head to last 32Scotland vs Brazil Highlights, FIFA World Cup 2026: Neymar's return rounded off a perfect day for Brazil as they made it to the round of 32 with a 3-0 win. Morocco overcame a scare from Haiti to make it to the next round.

Ben Stokes apologises to England teammates, urges focus on New Zealand series deciderENG vs NZ 3rd Test: Ben Stokes said he apologised to England teammates for the distraction caused by his curfew-breach controversy and urged the squad to focus on the series-deciding third Test against New Zealand at Trent Bridge.

Shafali Verma reveals the work behind her growing impact with the ballWomen's T20 World Cup, IND vs BAN: India opener Shafali Verma backed the team's ability to handle pressure, insisting the focus remains on Bangladesh despite a must-win situation. The all-rounder also embraced her growing bowling role, saying she now sees herself as an all-rounder.

FIFA WC Highlights: Switzerland, Canada head to knockouts while Bosnia waitFIFA World Cup 2026, Canada vs Switzerland, Bosnia vs Qatar Highlights: Switzerland secure a 2-1 win over Canada but both teams qualify for the Round of 32. Bosnia keep their hopes alive with a 3-1 victory against Qatar.

India's 2036 Olympics bid enters key phase as IOC revamps host selection processIndia's bid to host the 2036 Olympics received a clearer roadmap after the IOC approved a new Strategic Dialogue phase in the selection process. The host city will now be decided in mid-2029, with Ahmedabad among the leading contenders.

Dev Meena, Anushka Yadav shine on Inter-State C'ships, seal Asiad 2026 spotsAsian Games qualification took centre stage on Day 1 of the National Inter-State Championships, with multiple athletes achieving the required standards and Gulveer Singh claiming gold in the men's 5000m.

Worked hard on my bowling: Ind opener Shafali Verma on her growing all-round roleWomen's T20 World Cup 2026, IND vs BAN: Shafali Verma said preparation and visualisation have sharpened her value as India's part-time bowling option before the Bangladesh game. Her added role gives India balance as Harmanpreet Kaur's side confront a must-win stretch after the South Africa defeat.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
